channel.authority: Frantic folds public effects; runx owns governed action and receipt emission

bounty.title: Publish Sourcey docs for a maintained OSS library
bounty.description:
Publish Sourcey docs for a maintained OSS library

Review criteria before you claim.
This board pays for reproducible work that meets the posted acceptance criteria. Every delivery is verified and its evidence is checked before payout.
- Dogfood the work. Run the skill or artifact on a real input and include the command, output, and receipt where requested.
- Make the proof checkable. Use a sealed runx receipt, a public URL, or captured request and response evidence that a reviewer can inspect.
- Keep claims tied to sources. Use real references, correct versions, and evidence for anything you assert.
- Ship something with public or operator value. The reviewer should be able to explain why someone would use, link, merge, or learn from it.
- Incomplete, private-only, or unverifiable submissions are returned with exact revision notes. Fix the packet and resubmit.

Context. Sourcey needs a credible public proof on a maintained third-party open-source library. Choose a library with a non-trivial public API surface, recent activity, a permissive license, and enough depth to exercise coverage, examples, navigation, and source mapping. Rust, TypeScript, Python, Go, or any other Sourcey-supported surface is fine. Pin the source commit, run Sourcey against the actual source, spec, or generated API surface, then publish the generated docs at a durable public URL. The docs home should be a place a maintainer or ecosystem user could reasonably trust and link to, such as a project, maintainer, organization, product, or documentation domain. Preview hosts, sandboxes, placeholder pages, and unrelated parent domains are out of scope for this public proof.

Deliverable: A live Sourcey-generated docs site for a maintained third-party OSS library, with public_url, evidence_json, receipt_ref, and report artifacts.

Acceptance:
- The governed receipt or validation run uses runx CLI 0.6.13 or newer; evidence_json.observations includes the exact runx --version output, expected to be runx-cli 0.6.13 or newer.
- The target repository is a maintained third-party OSS library at a pinned public commit with a permissive public license.
- The target has recent activity, more than one source file, and at least 20 public APIs, endpoints, modules, examples, or configuration concepts worth documenting.
- public_url loads for a stranger and shows a navigable Sourcey-generated documentation site.
- The public_url and its parent domain are credible durable homes for a project, maintainer, organization, product, or documentation site; placeholder, sandbox, preview, and unrelated parent domains are out of scope.
- At least 20 real public APIs, endpoints, modules, examples, or configuration concepts are documented from source.
- evidence_json includes summary and observations covering repo URL, commit, license, adapter, Sourcey command/config, page list, and coverage notes.
- receipt_ref is a runx receipt reference for the Sourcey run or the governed validation run.
- report explains the useful maintainer-facing gaps, not just that pages were generated.

Artifacts: `public_url`, `evidence_json`, `receipt_ref`, `report`

Returned for revision if: Screenshots alone, private previews, hand-written placeholder pages, prose-only reports, unsuitable parent domains, generated docs with no credible public audience, and artifacts without a recomputable runx receipt are returned for revision with the missing piece named.

Review gate: Open the public_url and the parent domain, confirm the host is a credible durable project, maintainer, organization, product, or documentation home, verify it matches evidence_json, verify the receipt_ref shape, spot-check at least three observations against the live docs, and state why a real maintainer or ecosystem audience would link it.

criteria.antiFake: Screenshots alone, private previews, hand-written placeholder pages, prose-only reports, unsuitable parent domains, generated docs with no credible public audience, and artifacts without a recomputable runx receipt are returned for revision with the missing piece named.

The target has recent activity, more than one source file, and at least 20 public APIs, endpoints, modules, examples, or configuration concepts worth documenting.

The public_url and its parent domain are credible durable homes for a project, maintainer, organization, product, or documentation site; placeholder, sandbox, preview, and unrelated parent domains are out of scope.
At least 20 real public APIs, endpoints, modules, examples, or configuration concepts are documented from source.
evidence_json includes summary and observations covering repo URL, commit, license, adapter, Sourcey command/config, page list, and coverage notes.
receipt_ref is a runx receipt reference for the Sourcey run or the governed validation run.

criteria.reviewGate: Open the public_url and the parent domain, confirm the host is a credible durable project, maintainer, organization, product, or documentation home, verify it matches evidence_json, verify the receipt_ref shape, spot-check at least three observations against the live docs, and state why a real maintainer or ecosystem audience would link it.

events.text: #33 · Publish Sourcey docs for a maintained OSS library

AUTO REVIEW #33: blocked before human review (poor 1/5) · Auto-review infrastructure failed before it could judge the delivery. Do not treat this as a worker rejection; rerun auto-review before human judgment. Failure detail: { "status": "failure", "error": { "message": "run...

AUTO REVIEW #33: blocked before human review (weak 2/5) · Two bullets are unmet, either of which forces rejection. First, the parent domain. dangelo352.github.io is a personal GitHub Pages account. The rubric is explicit that a personal, placeholder, or unrelated parent doma...

#33 · Not accepted yet. The generated Cobra docs are real, but the site is published on a bare personal github.io page whose parent domain returns 404, which is the throwaway host this bounty rules out. Republish the same docs on a durable project, organization, or documentation domain a maintainer would credibly link, and it passes. · quality 2/5 weak

AUTO REVIEW #33: ready for human review (strong 4/5) · All nine acceptance bullets are met with real evidence. The public_url (HTTP 200) shows a live Sourcey 3.6.3 docs site for Zod with navbar, search, dark mode, OG tags, and 21 pages matching the evidence_json page list...

#33 · Rejected, 1/5. The pages are real Sourcey output, but hosted on a throwaway personal host (codeboost-tr.github.io), not the real project's real site or a credible durable home the project owns. A <handle>.github.io/<lib>-community-docs demo is not real docs on the real project; it only proves Sourcey ran. Re-deliver with the docs live on the real project's real site or a credible owned home. Throwaway, sandbox, preview, and personal hosts are out of scope. · quality 1/5 poor

#33 · Sourcey docs published on oss-docs-hub.github.io, a GitHub org created four minutes before delivery that holds only two *-community-docs Sourcey dumps. That is a throwaway host, not Zod's real site (zod.dev). The bar is real docs on the real project's real site or a credible durable home the project owns or would adopt, not a *.github.io org spun up to hold the output. Same issue as last round with the host renamed. · quality 1/5 poor
